questions.explanation: DevOps focuses on collaboration, automation, and feedback loops to improve delivery speed and reliability.

options.A: Increase collaboration and automate delivery to ship changes faster and more reliably
options.C: Eliminate the need for testing by deploying directly to production
options.D: Move all applications to a single cloud provider

questions.question: What is the primary goal of DevOps in a software delivery organization?

questions.explanation: git checkout -b <branch> creates the branch and checks it out in a single command.
questions.question: In Git, which command creates a new branch named "feature/login" and switches to it in one step?

questions.explanation: top provides an interactive, real-time view of processes and system resource usage.
questions.question: On a Linux system, which command is commonly used to view real-time CPU and memory usage interactively?

questions.explanation: CMD defines the default command executed when the container starts (can be overridden at runtime).
questions.question: Which Dockerfile instruction is primarily used to specify the command that runs when a container starts?

questions.explanation: Restricting deployment to the main branch ensures PR/MR validation runs without deploying, while merges to main can deploy.

options.A: Run deploy job only when the pipeline is triggered by a schedule
options.B: Run deploy job only when the branch is main (and not for PR/MR pipelines)
options.C: Run deploy job only when tests fail
options.D: Run deploy job only when the commit message contains [deploy]

questions.question: In a CI pipeline, you want to prevent deployments from running on pull request builds but allow them on merges to the main branch. Which condition best matches this requirement?

questions.explanation: A remote backend centralizes state and supports locking to prevent concurrent writes and state corruption.

options.A: Store terraform.tfstate in the application container image
options.B: Store terraform.tfstate in each engineer’s home directory
options.C: Use a remote backend with state locking (e.g., S3 + DynamoDB, or Terraform Cloud)
options.D: Disable state entirely and rely on provider APIs at runtime

questions.question: In Terraform, what is the best practice for storing the state file for a team to avoid conflicts and enable locking?

questions.explanation: A Service provides a stable virtual IP/DNS name and load-balances traffic to matching Pods.
questions.question: In Kubernetes, which object is typically used to expose a set of Pods internally and provide stable service discovery via a DNS name?

questions.explanation: Using a secrets manager with identity-based access avoids committing secrets to Git and reduces plaintext exposure in manifests.

options.A: Hardcode the password in the container image as an environment variable
options.B: Store the password in a ConfigMap and mount it as a file
options.C: Retrieve the secret at runtime from a secrets manager (e.g., Vault/AWS SSM) using workload identity/IAM and inject it into the pod
options.D: Commit the password to Git in an encrypted file and decrypt it manually on the cluster

questions.question: Your Kubernetes app needs a database password. You must avoid storing secrets in Git and also avoid putting plaintext secrets into Kubernetes manifests. Which approach best meets this requirement?

questions.explanation: Distributed tracing (Jaeger) correlates spans across services to identify where latency is introduced in a request flow.
questions.question: You are investigating intermittent latency spikes in a microservices system. Metrics show increased request duration, but logs don’t clearly show where time is spent across services. Which observability tool is most appropriate to pinpoint the slow hop in the request path?

questions.explanation: Multi-stage builds allow compiling/installing in a builder stage and producing a smaller runtime image with only what’s needed for production.

options.A: Use a single-stage image based on a full OS image and run npm install without flags
options.B: Use multi-stage builds: install dependencies/build in a builder stage, then copy only built artifacts and production dependencies into a slim runtime stage
options.C: Copy the entire project including node_modules from your laptop into the image
options.D: Run the app directly on the host without containers to reduce image size

questions.question: You are building a Node.js API using a common software framework. You want to run it in production with minimal image size and avoid shipping dev dependencies. Which Docker approach best fits?
